description:
Long-term, shared agent memory exposed as tools, backed by the hosted Mem0 Platform.
Exposes two agent tools: 'remember' stores conversation turns and 'recall' returns the relevant memories. Set your API key and a user id and it works; point several agents at the same project (and ids) to share one memory.

agent_id.description: Optional agent scope. Caution: on the hosted Mem0 platform an agent_id is not reliably stored on the memory but still narrows recall, so setting it can make recalls come back empty. Prefer scoping by user id; leave this empty unless you have verified it works.
agent_id.title: Agent id

app_id.description: Optional app scope. Same caveat as Agent id: it narrows recall but is not reliably stored on the hosted platform, so setting it can hide memories. Prefer scoping by user id.
app_id.title: App id

base_url.description: Mem0 REST API base URL. Defaults to the hosted endpoint; override for self-hosted / enterprise deployments.
base_url.title: Base URL

infer.description: When on (default), Mem0 runs LLM extraction to derive salient facts from the turns. When off, the raw messages are stored verbatim.
infer.title: Infer memories

ingest_timeout.description: Max seconds 'remember' waits/polls for background extraction to finish before returning a 'queued' result.
ingest_timeout.title: Store timeout (s)

run_id.description: Optional session/run scope stamped on stored memories and used to narrow recall. Can be passed by the agent per call.
run_id.title: Run id

search_limit.description: Maximum number of memory rows to retrieve per recall.
search_limit.title: Recall limit

wait.description: When on (default), 'remember' polls Mem0 until background extraction finishes, so a following 'recall' can see the new memory. When off, 'remember' returns immediately and extraction runs in the background.
wait.title: Synchronous store

api_key.description: Mem0 Platform API key (m0-...). Get it from the Mem0 dashboard: app.mem0.ai → API Keys.
api_key.title: API Key

show_advanced.description: Show advanced options. The defaults work for most cases, leave off for a simple setup.
show_advanced.title: Advanced settings

user_id.description: Who this memory belongs to (used to store and recall). Can be passed by the agent per call. At least one scope (user id, or an advanced agent/run/app id) is required.
user_id.title: User id

title: Mem0
